attrs.alt: Access reviews scope types (groups, apps, roles, access packages) and decision flow with approve/deny outcomes and ML recommendations

content.text: Access reviews in Entra ID Governance are certification campaigns where assigned reviewers confirm that specified users still need access to a resource. Reviews can target four resource types: group or Teams membership, enterprise app assignments, Entra directory role assignments, and access package assignments (configured via the package policy). Unlike PIM alerts which react to violations, access reviews proactively validate that existing access remains appropriate over time.
Compliance use case: SOC 2, ISO 27001, and FedRAMP controls require periodic recertification that users' access is still authorized. Access reviews produce a reviewable audit trail.
Drift correction: users change teams, projects end, contractors finish — but group memberships and app assignments are rarely cleaned up automatically. Regular reviews catch this drift.
Least-privilege enforcement: reviews force reviewers to actively justify access rather than relying on the absence of a removal request.

When creating a review, the scope controls which principals and which resources are included. Getting scope right is the most important configuration decision — too broad and reviewers have thousands of items to review; too narrow and violations slip through.
Quarterly review of a sensitive security group like 'prod-access'
Large-scale annual review across all groups
Monthly review of a privileged SaaS app like Salesforce Admin
Quarterly review of Global Administrator and Security Administrator
Focus review on users who haven't signed in for 90+ days

For enterprise app reviews, the scope targets a service principal's app role assignments. This is the right review type for SaaS applications where only certain users should have access, or where specific app roles (Admin vs Read-only) need to be validated.
Single user OIDs in reviewers array
Small groups where a known security officer reviews everything
Delegate review to a security team group — any member can process decisions
Large-scale reviews where managers certify their direct reports' access
App owners certify who should have access to their application
Low-risk groups where users confirm they still need access
Used when primary reviewer (manager) does not exist — prevents no-reviewer gaps

Always configure fallbackReviewers when using manager-based review. If a user has no manager attribute set in Entra, there will be no reviewer for that entry — it will time out and hit defaultDecision. A fallback (e.g., the security team) catches these orphan cases.
The settings block controls what happens when a reviewer acts (or fails to act) and when the review period closes. These are the most operationally significant settings.
Applied to any entry the reviewer did not act on before the review closes. Deny forces reviewers to respond or lose access.
When true, denied entries have their access removed automatically at review close. When false, a Privileged Role Admin must manually trigger apply.
Shows AI-generated Approve/Deny suggestions to reviewers based on sign-in history. Reduces time per decision on large review sets.
How far back to look for sign-in activity when computing recommendations. Default 30 days.
Forces reviewers to type a reason when approving. Creates compliance evidence trail.
How many days each review instance is open. Shorter = tighter controls; longer = more time for reviewers.

# Get all review definitions in the tenant
$defs = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions'
Write-Host "Total review definitions: $($defs.value.Count)"

# Get the latest instance of a specific review
$defId = '<review-definition-id>'
$instances = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions/$defId/instances"
$latest = $instances.value | Sort-Object startDateTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
Write-Host "Status: $($latest.status)  End: $($latest.endDateTime)"

# Enumerate all decisions in the instance
$decisions = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions/$defId/instances/$($latest.id)/decisions"

$decisions.value | Select-Object `
    @{N='User';       E={$_.principal.displayName}},
    @{N='Decision';   E={$_.decision}},
    @{N='Reviewer';   E={$_.reviewedBy.displayName}},
    @{N='Reason';     E={$_.justification}},
    @{N='Applied';    E={$_.applyResult}}

# Export to CSV for audit submission
$decisions.value | Select-Object @{N="User";E={$_.principal.userPrincipalName}},decision,justification,reviewedDateTime |
    Export-Csv -Path '~/access-review-export.csv' -NoTypeInformation
Stopping and Restarting a Review Instance
A running review instance can be stopped early if all decisions are complete, or reset if reviewers need to start over. Stopping a review in 'InProgress' state triggers autoApply if configured.

# Stop an in-progress review instance early
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions/$defId/instances/$($latest.id)/stop"

# Reset all decisions in an instance (allows reviewers to re-decide)
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions/$defId/instances/$($latest.id)/resetDecisions"

# Manually apply decisions after review closes (when autoApply is false)
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
  -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions/$defId/instances/$($latest.id)/applyDecisions"
Entra access review events flow into the audit log under 'AccessReview' operations. Route to Log Analytics for long-term retention and KQL-based compliance dashboards.

// Access review decisions over last 90 days grouped by outcome
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where LoggedByService == 'Access Reviews'
| where OperationName in ('Review decision recorded', 'Apply access review result')
| extend Decision = tostring(AdditionalDetails[?(@.key=='decision')].value)
| extend Resource = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| summarize Count=count() by Decision, Resource
| order by Count desc

// Users removed via access review in last 30 days
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName == 'Apply access review result'
| where Result == 'success'
| extend RemovedUser = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
| extend Resource    = tostring(TargetResources[1].displayName)
| project TimeGenerated, RemovedUser, Resource, OperationName

Access reviews support multi-stage configuration where different sets of reviewers act in sequence. Stage 1 might be the resource owner making initial decisions; stage 2 might be a compliance officer who reviews and overrides stage 1 decisions where needed. Multi-stage reviews require the Entra ID P2 or Microsoft Entra ID Governance license.
Set decisionHistoriesForReviewersEnabled=true in multi-stage reviews so stage 2 reviewers can see what stage 1 decided and their justifications. Without this, stage 2 reviewers act blind.

A common high-value review pattern targets only users who have not signed in within a configurable period. This focuses reviewer attention on the highest-risk subset — accounts that are assigned access but may be orphaned, belonging to users who changed roles, left the company, or are service accounts with no legitimate owner.

External B2B guest users are a common source of access drift. Guests are often invited for a specific project or event and then forgotten. Access reviews can target all guest users in the tenant, or only guest members of specific groups, giving you a systematic way to recertify or remove guest access.
Guest removed from group; guest account remains in tenant
Deny (guest has no other group memberships)
Guest account stays; you must separately block sign-in or delete via Lifecycle Workflows
Same as explicit Deny — membership removed

Access review removal only affects the specific resource reviewed (group membership, app assignment). It does not delete the guest account. Use Lifecycle Workflows leaver tasks or a scheduled cleanup script to delete stale guest accounts after their last review denial.
Entries for users with no manager attribute are never reviewed; all hit defaultDecision silently
Always set fallbackReviewers to a security team group
Non-responses result in no action — stale access persists indefinitely
Set defaultDecision=Deny or Recommendation for all reviews of sensitive resources
Review completes but no access is revoked — a manual apply step is silently missed
Enable auto-apply, or build a Logic App that calls applyDecisions when a review instance completes
Reviewers have 500+ items; skip-clicking leads to meaningless approvals
Narrow scope to specific groups or roles; target inactive users only; use multi-stage
Emergency account removed from Global Admin because it had no recent sign-ins
Exclude break-glass accounts from review scope using a group exclusion filter
Guest review removes group but account persists
Removed guest can still sign in to the tenant and appear in other queries
Pair with Lifecycle Workflows leaver flow or scheduled account cleanup script
Access reviews in Entra ID Governance recertify that users still need the access they hold — covering groups, enterprise apps, Entra directory roles, and access package assignments.

Set defaultDecision=Deny so reviewers who fail to respond cause access to be removed rather than silently retained. Enable autoApplyDecisionsEnabled to remove denied access automatically without a manual apply step.
ML recommendations (recommendationsEnabled=true) show reviewers whether each user has been active recently, reducing time-to-decision on large review sets.
Always configure fallbackReviewers when using manager-based review to handle users without a manager attribute — otherwise those entries time out and hit defaultDecision without human review.
Review events appear in the Entra audit log under the 'Access Reviews' service. Export decisions to CSV or query via KQL for compliance evidence.
Access review definitions are reusable — one definition drives recurring quarterly or monthly instances. Decisions from each instance are individually queryable via the Graph API.
